source.title: Infants' spontaneous movements explore arm dynamics

confidence_rationale: Nature Communications Biology empirical study using RGB-D motion capture (n=20 infants, 270+ spontaneous movement units) with synthetic comparison datasets. Directly measures kinematic space coverage and acceleration distributions. High source quality; mapping fidelity is strong because the finding -- that spontaneous movements are optimized for maximal dynamic excitation, not goal achievement -- directly supports the REE claim that action diversity generation precedes and bootstraps capability formation. Transfer risk low because human infant data.
failure_signatures: Analysis is confined to arm movement; it is unclear whether the same dynamic-excitation optimization logic extends to lower-limb or whole-body locomotor exploration.
Comparison was against reaching-like motions, not other spontaneous movement types; the design assumes a clear spontaneous/goal-directed dichotomy that may not be clean in naturalistic settings.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Infant spontaneous movements engage arm dynamics more extensively than reaching-like motions, with acceleration distributions skewed toward trajectories that maximize dynamic excitation; the kinematic space explored has significantly higher variability than goal-directed reaching.
mapping.ree_translation: In REE terms, the infant exploration phase is not random noise nor degraded reaching -- it is a systematic probe of the action space's dynamic range. This directly supports ARC-065 (behavioral diversity generation is a distinct functional phase) and INV-073 (developmental bootstrapping is a universal prerequisite for capability acquisition). An agent that skips exploration cannot map the corners of its own action space.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The study quantifies kinematic richness but does not measure what representational structure is built from this exploration. The REE claim that exploration builds a behavioral option store remains an inference, not a direct observation.
mapping.source_context: Human infants aged approximately 3-6 months; RGB-D motion capture + YouTube video corpus; synthetic data comparison using inverse dynamics models.

confidence_components.notes: Strong empirical design with direct kinematic measurement; minor mapping gap between kinematic richness and representational structure.
